title: Self-Improving Module Launched
summary: SwarmAI now reserves 10% of every workflow's token budget for automatic framework improvement. The Community Investment Ledger tracks collective impact across all deployments.
content:
The new `swarmai-self-improving` module introduces a first-of-its-kind mechanism: every workflow automatically invests 10% of its token budget into discovering framework-level improvements. These aren't per-workflow optimizations — they produce generic rules that ship in the next release and benefit all users on upgrade.

The module includes an ImprovementCollector, PatternExtractor, ImprovementClassifier, and a three-tier pipeline (auto-merge, PR review, architecture proposals). A Community Investment Ledger tracks total tokens invested, improvements shipped, skills graduated, and ROI.

For firewalled environments, the ImprovementExporter produces anonymized JSON files that ops teams can submit through intelliswarm.ai/contribute.

title: GitHub Improvement Reporting Pipeline
summary: Connected deployments can now automatically create GitHub PRs with evidence-backed improvement proposals. Firewalled environments get health indicators, log nudges, and export tools.
content:
The reporting pipeline adds two channels for improvements to flow back to the framework repository:

**Channel 1: GitHub PRs** — When a GitHub token is configured, the framework automatically creates branches and pull requests with full evidence: what changes, cross-validation results, expected impact, and who benefits on upgrade. Tier 1 improvements auto-merge if CI passes; Tier 2 requires human review.

**Channel 2: Telemetry** — Anonymized improvement data is sent to the central endpoint for aggregation across all deployments worldwide.

For air-gapped environments: Spring Actuator health indicators, scheduled log nudges, and startup banners remind ops teams about pending improvements. The ImprovementExporter produces security-auditable JSON files.

title: Contribute Portal Live on IntelliSwarm.ai
summary: Enterprise teams running SwarmAI behind firewalls can now submit improvement exports through the website at intelliswarm.ai/contribute, via GitHub issues, email, or CLI.
content:
The new /contribute page on intelliswarm.ai provides a drag-and-drop upload interface for SwarmAI improvement export files. The page validates the JSON format, shows a breakdown of improvements by tier, and submits them to the backend for aggregation.

Four submission channels are now available:
1. **Web form** — Upload at intelliswarm.ai/contribute
2. **GitHub issue** — Using the improvement-contribution template
3. **Email** — Send to contributions@intelliswarm.ai
4. **CLI** — curl directly to the API endpoint

All exports contain only anonymized structural patterns — no workflow content, no user data, no proprietary information.

title: Enterprise Module: Multi-Tenancy & Deep RL
summary: The swarmai-enterprise module adds license-gated multi-tenancy, advanced governance, and DQN neural networks for reinforcement learning-powered decision making.
content:
The enterprise module brings production-grade features for large-scale deployments:

- **Multi-tenancy** — Tenant-isolated memory, knowledge bases, quotas, and budgets
- **Advanced governance** — Fine-grained RBAC with tool permission levels (READ_ONLY, WORKSPACE_WRITE, DANGEROUS)
- **Deep RL** — DQN neural networks for policy optimization, replacing the tabular Q-learning in the core module
- **Audit logging** — Complete decision trail for compliance requirements

Activated with a BSL license key via `swarmai.enterprise.license-key` configuration.

title: Self-Evaluation Module for Continuous Quality
summary: The swarmai-eval module provides a framework for agentic self-evaluation, competitive benchmarks against LangGraph and CrewAI, and continuous quality improvement metrics.
content:
SwarmAI now includes built-in evaluation capabilities:

- **Agentic self-evaluation** — Workflows assess their own output quality, convergence efficiency, and tool selection accuracy
- **Competitive benchmarks** — Standardized comparison against LangGraph, CrewAI, and AutoGen on token efficiency, quality, and governance compliance
- **Continuous metrics** — Track quality trends across versions to ensure improvements from the 10% investment are validated

Results are available at intelliswarm.ai/benchmarks.

title: 1,000+ Tests Passing
summary: SwarmAI crossed 1,013 passing tests across all modules, covering process types, governance, budget tracking, skill generation, and the new self-improvement pipeline.
content:
The test suite now covers:
- All 7 process types with end-to-end workflow tests
- Governance gate approval/rejection flows
- Budget enforcement (HARD_STOP and WARN modes)
- Dynamic skill generation and graduation
- Self-improvement pipeline: collection, extraction, classification, aggregation
- GitHub and telemetry reporting
- Enterprise multi-tenancy isolation

Every self-improvement proposal must pass the full suite before shipping. This is the safety net that makes automatic Tier 1 improvements possible.
